page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, utilizing an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is network-based with a local capacity of 1 GB, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results show a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance with a score of 3612.58, which is mirrored by top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression rates, such as 39.78 GB/sec in gzip and 17.59 GB/sec in bzip3. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng falls into the average category at 14434.3, likely due to the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth tests also place the instance in the average tier, with read-only large block speeds at 72.12 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 41.41 GB/sec.

For language model workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 324.23 tokens/sec on small 135M models, but exhibits weak prompt processing performance of 27.21 tokens/sec on medium 7B models. Database operations and static web serving throughput both align with average tier benchmarks, registering 5428.8 in PassMark database operations and 26.44 GB/sec in web serving. Given the shared CPU allocation and network-based storage, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight background processing rather than sustained high-performance multi-threaded computing.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 shared vCPUs on 4 physical AMD EPYC cores and 32.0 GB of RAM. It includes 1 GB of network-based local storage and one public IPv4 address, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3612.58 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 39.78 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. While the server delivers strong text generation speeds for small language models, it shows weak prompt processing for medium 7B models. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core compute resources.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and network storage. It offers top-tier single-core performance but average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and network-based storage.
tagline: An x86_64 shared-core virtual server combining top-tier single-threaded performance with balanced memory for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Network-based local storage with 1 GB capacity and one public IPv4 address
Suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and database operations
